highlightedText:
Imagine this: you're about to embark on an exciting journey to one of the most captivating cities in the world—Copenhagen (哥本哈根) ! Now, you are preparing for this trip.

You need to list 6 interesting spots in Copenhagen as the potential destinations, as well as the reason for choosing them, the admission prices, and the opening days, and list 6 important “travel tips” about Copenhagen.

To better evaluate the result, please organize your answers according to the following format:

Example:
	Location 1:
	location: UCLA
	reason: it's a famous university
	price: free
	opening days: all days
Location 2:

	...

text: "Hello" in Danish is "hej", "thank you" is "farvel", and "goodbye" is "tak".
The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen.
The weather in Copenhagen in December is below -15 degrees Celsius.
There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro.
The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros.
The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K.
It's one of the most significant landmarks in Copenhagen, both historically and politically.

Free
Opening days: Tuesday to Sunday
It's a world-famous restaurant offering culinary masterpieces.
It's a statue of the character from Grimm's
It's the home of the Danish royal family. You can witness the change of guard at 12:00.
t's a famous example of modern architecture.
